# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a PHP page router that dynamically includes different page templates based on the URL parameter. Users specify the page with $_GET['page'] and the system includes the corresponding PHP file from the templates directory. Keep it simple and flexible for our multi-page website CMS.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Canonicalize and validate file paths to prevent path traversal.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Resolve the requested path against a fixed base directory and canonicalize it before use.
- Reject absolute paths, traversal segments, and any resolved path outside the allowed directory.
- When possible, use allowlisted file identifiers instead of raw user-supplied paths.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not join a user filename directly to a base directory and open it without validation.
- Do not rely only on checking whether ".." appears in the string.
# Category: path_traversal

<?php

/**
 * Secure PHP Page Router
 * 
 * Dynamically includes page templates based on URL parameter
 * with strict path traversal prevention.
 */

// Configuration
define('TEMPLATES_DIR', __DIR__ . '/templates');
define('DEFAULT_PAGE', 'home');
define('TEMPLATE_EXTENSION', '.php');

/**
 * Allowlist of valid page identifiers.
 * 
 * This is the primary security mechanism: only pages explicitly listed here
 * can be loaded. Add new pages to this array as they are created.
 */
$allowlisted_pages = [
    'home',
    'about',
    'contact',
    'services',
    'portfolio',
    'blog',
    'faq',
    'privacy',
    'terms',
    '404',
];

/**
 * Validates and resolves a page identifier to a safe file path.
 *
 * @param string $page_identifier The user-supplied page name.
 * @param array  $allowlist       The list of permitted page identifiers.
 * @return string|false The resolved, validated file path or false on failure.
 */
function resolve_template_path(string $page_identifier, array $allowlist)
{
    // Step 1: Check against the allowlist of known page identifiers.
    // This is the strongest defense — only explicitly permitted identifiers pass.
    if (!in_array($page_identifier, $allowlist, true)) {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 2: Validate the identifier format as an additional safeguard.
    // Only allow alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores.
    // This prevents any sneaky encoding or special characters even if
    // someone manages to inject into the allowlist.
    if (!preg_match('/^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$/', $page_identifier)) {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 3: Construct the candidate file path.
    $candidate_path = TEMPLATES_DIR . DIRECTORY_SEPARATOR . $page_identifier . TEMPLATE_EXTENSION;

    // Step 4: Canonicalize the path using realpath() to resolve any symlinks,
    // relative segments, or other filesystem tricks.
    $real_path = realpath($candidate_path);

    if ($real_path === false) {
        // File does not exist on disk.
        return false;
    }

    // Step 5: Canonicalize the base directory as well.
    $real_base_dir = realpath(TEMPLATES_DIR);

    if ($real_base_dir === false) {
        // Templates directory itself doesn't exist — configuration error.
        error_log('Page Router: Templates directory does not exist: ' . TEMPLATES_DIR);
        return false;
    }

    // Ensure the base directory path ends with a directory separator
    // to prevent prefix-matching attacks (e.g., /templates-evil/).
    $real_base_dir_prefix = $real_base_dir . DIRECTORY_SEPARATOR;

    // Step 6: Verify the resolved path is strictly within the templates directory.
    if (strpos($real_path, $real_base_dir_prefix) !== 0) {
        // The resolved file is outside the allowed directory — reject it.
        error_log('Page Router: Path traversal attempt detected. Resolved path: ' . $real_path);
        return false;
    }

    // Step 7: Verify the file is a regular file (not a directory, device, etc.).
    if (!is_file($real_path)) {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 8: Verify the file is readable.
    if (!is_readable($real_path)) {
        error_log('Page Router: Template file is not readable: ' . $real_path);
        return false;
    }

    return $real_path;
}

/**
 * Renders a 404 error page.
 */
function render_404_page(array $allowlist): void
{
    http_response_code(404);

    $error_template = resolve_template_path('404', $allowlist);
    if ($error_template !== false) {
        include $error_template;
    } else {
        // Fallback if no 404 template exists.
        echo '<!DOCTYPE html><html><head><title>404 - Page Not Found</title></head>';
        echo '<body><h1>404 - Page Not Found</h1>';
        echo '<p>The requested page could not be found.</p>';
        echo '<p><a href="?page=home">Return to Home</a></p>';
        echo '</body></html>';
    }
}

// ============================================================================
// Main Router Logic
// ============================================================================

// Retrieve the requested page from the query string.
$requested_page = isset($_GET['page']) ? $_GET['page'] : DEFAULT_PAGE;

// Ensure the input is a string (defense against array injection).
if (!is_string($requested_page)) {
    $requested_page = DEFAULT_PAGE;
}

// Trim whitespace.
$requested_page = trim($requested_page);

// If empty after trimming, use default.
if ($requested_page === '') {
    $requested_page = DEFAULT_PAGE;
}

// Normalize to lowercase for consistent matching.
$requested_page = strtolower($requested_page);

// Resolve the template path securely.
$template_path = resolve_template_path($requested_page, $allowlisted_pages);

if ($template_path !== false) {
    // Valid page found — include it.
    // Make the current page identifier available to the template.
    $current_page = $requested_page;
    include $template_path;
} else {
    // Invalid or non-existent page — show 404.
    render_404_page($allowlisted_pages);
}
